name: San Caprisio, Cuevas de San Caprisio, y Alcubierre  – circular por la Sierra de Alcubierre
region_intro: Mountain biking around Alcubierre offers diverse trails through the Sierra de Alcubierre, a mountain range characterized by varied terrain. The region features a mix of forested areas, expansive views, and open landscapes. Trails range from steady gradient dirt tracks to uneven road surfaces, with some sections presenting dusty, loose ground and technical features. This landscape provides a suitable environment for mountain biking across different skill levels. Best mountain bike trails around Alcubierre The most popular mountain bike trail is Farlete, Camino Real de Zaragoza, y Torre Ventosa — paseo por el sur de los Monegros, a 21.4 miles (34.4 km) trail that takes about 3 hours 21 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the southern Monegros region. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is San Caprisio, Cuevas de San Caprisio, y Alcubierre – circular por la Sierra de Alcubierre, a moderate 24.7 miles (39.8 km) path. This circular route passes through the Sierra de Alcubierre, offering views of the natural caves of San Caprasio. Local mountain bikers also love the Leciñena, San Caprasio, y Estación meteorlógica — circular por los Monegros, a 35.0 miles (56.3 km) trail leading through the Monegros and past Monte Oscuro, often completed in about 5 hours 24 minutes. Mountain biking around Alcubierre is defined by the Sierra de Alcubierre mountain range, varied terrain including dirt tracks and technical features, and panoramic views from elevated points like Monte Oscuro. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to challenging routes requiring advanced skills. The routes in Alcubierre are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 5 reviews. More than 100 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Alcubierre's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in Alcubierre?
A: Alcubierre offers a wide selection of mountain biking experiences, with over 30 routes available. These range from moderate paths to more challenging trails, catering to various skill levels.

Q: Are there any mountain bike trails suitable for beginners or those looking for a less challenging ride?
A: While there are no routes explicitly classified as 'easy' in our current selection, many of the moderate trails can be suitable for less experienced riders. These routes often feature steady gradient dirt tracks. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1147724824/farlete-camino-real-de-zaragoza-y-torre-ventosa-paseo-por-el-sur-de-los-monegros" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Farlete, Camino Real de Zaragoza, y Torre Ventosa — paseo por el sur de los Monegros&lt;/a&gt; route is a moderate 21.4-mile trail that explores the southern Monegros region.

Q: Which mountain bike trails offer the most challenging experience in Alcubierre?
A: For experienced mountain bikers seeking a challenge, Alcubierre features several difficult routes. One notable option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1147673767/lecinena-san-caprasio-y-estacion-meteorlogica-circular-por-los-monegros" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Leciñena, San Caprasio, y Estación meteorlógica — circular por los Monegros&lt;/a&gt;, a 35.0-mile trail with significant elevation gain, often completed in about 5 hours 24 minutes. These trails can include dusty, loose ground and super technical steep features.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in the Alcubierre area?
A: Yes, Alcubierre offers several excellent circular mountain bike routes. A popular choice is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e976123273/san-caprisio-cuevas-de-san-caprisio-y-alcubierre-circular-por-la-sierra-de-alcubierre" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Caprisio, Cuevas de San Caprisio, y Alcubierre – circular por la Sierra de Alcubierre&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 24.7-mile path that takes you through the Sierra de Alcubierre and past the natural caves of San Caprasio.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the mountain bike trails in Alcubierre?
A: The trails in Alcubierre feature varied terrain. You'll encounter everything from steady gradient dirt tracks and uneven road surfaces to areas with dusty, loose ground and fierce slate rock. Some sections can be super technical and steep, requiring advanced riding skills.

Q: What historical or cultural sites can I explore while mountain biking in Alcubierre?
A: Alcubierre is rich in history. You can ride the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1147787845/orwell-route-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Orwell Route Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which features recreated fortifications from the Spanish Civil War, allowing you to experience a piece of history. The trenches and bunkers along this route appear untouched by time. For more information on the historical context, you can visit &lt;a href="https://www.atlasobscura.com/places/the-george-orwell-route-alcubierre-spain" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The George Orwell Route&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Which trails offer the best panoramic views in Alcubierre?
A: Many routes in Alcubierre provide spectacular views. A challenging but rewarding climb leads to the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5300059" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monte Oscuro Weather Radar&lt;/a&gt;, offering breathtaking panoramic views of the entire Cinca Medio region, the Pyrenees to the north, and the Monegros to the south. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5753138" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Enebro Peak&lt;/a&gt; is another excellent viewpoint.

Q: Are there any unique natural attractions to see along the mountain bike trails?
A: Absolutely. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5268339" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Caprasio Caves&lt;/a&gt;, located on the slopes of Mount San Caprasio, are a highlight. These natural caves hold historical and mystical significance, adding an ancestral touch to any route in the area. The hermitage of San Caprasio itself is carved into the rock.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in Alcubierre?
A: The mountain bike trails in Alcubierre are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars. Riders often praise the diverse terrain, the blend of natural beauty and historical sites, and the panoramic views from elevated points like Monte Oscuro.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Alcubierre?
A: Alcubierre is an excellent destination for outdoor activities. Given the varied terrain, which can include dusty and loose sections, the spring and autumn months generally offer the most pleasant conditions for mountain biking, avoiding the intense heat of summer and potential winter precipitation.

Q: Are there any religious or spiritual sites accessible by mountain bike?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5300070" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of the Virgin of the Sabina&lt;/a&gt;, situated on a hill outside Alcolea de Cinca, is a rural shrine surrounded by pine and juniper trees. It offers views overlooking the Cinca River and the Monegros mountains and is an ideal point to include on mountain biking routes.

Q: How long do mountain bike routes in Alcubierre typically take to complete?
A: The duration of routes varies depending on their length and difficulty. For example, the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17545658" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Orwell's Trenches – Posición San Simón loop from Albergue del Santuario de Magallón&lt;/a&gt;, which is about 24.5 miles (39.5 km), typically takes around 3 hours 37 minutes. More challenging routes can take over 5 hours.
tips: These caves, carved out of the rock, were restored in the 1950s by the Brotherhood of Jesus, which makes them available for walkers and spiritual retreats. Previously it is said that they were used by the bandit "Cucaracha" who frequented this area and hid in the cave where the hermitage is today.

We are at the viewpoint that bears the name of the hermitage of San Caprasio. From here we will have spectacular views of the Ebro valley and other regions that we can distinguish on the information panel.

Legend has it that San Caprasio was a shepherd (probably of goats, given his name) in the Sierra de Guara. One day he decided to become a monk and threw his staff very far, going to fall here, in the Sierra de Alcubierre.

The church of Santa Ana was built in the 14th century and is known for its Gothic-Renaissance tower and its Mudejar-style tower.

Unfortunately, in the Civil War, all the archives relevant to the history of the church were lost.
